title: Aldama grahamii
intro: Aldama grahamii, also known as Graham's Aldama, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Aldama grahamii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Aldama grahamii is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and relationships with other species. The genus Aldama is a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to the Americas.
